metadata.title: ggen v26.5.28 — Process Automation Definition of Done
metadata.description: Complete specification of all 10 automation dimensions preventing manual errors and enforcing gates

automation_dimensions.title: Git Hooks (Pre-Commit & Pre-Push Enforcement)
automation_dimensions.what_it_is: Bash scripts that run locally before committing and pushing code to main branch. Pre-commit is a fast tier gate; pre-push is comprehensive.
automation_dimensions.why_it_matters: Prevents broken code from entering the repository. Pre-commit catches 62% of defects in <10 seconds. Pre-push catches 97% of defects in <90 seconds. Without these gates, commits with compilation errors reach CI.
trigger_points: Pre-commit: After `git commit` on main branch
Pre-push: Before `git push origin main`
Triggered on: Changes to any tracked file in the repository
what_it_validates: Pre-commit (Fast Tier):
- Compilation check (Gate 1): `just check` — catches 60% of defects
- Format check (Gate 2): `just fmt-check` — ensures code formatting consistency
Pre-push (Full Tier):
- Check (Gate 1): `just check` — compiler errors, 60% of defects
- Lint (Gate 2): `just lint` — Clippy warnings, 15% of defects
- Format check (Gate 3): `just fmt-check` — 2% of defects
- Unit tests (Gate 4): `just test-lib` — 20% of defects
recovery: If pre-commit fails: Run `just check` locally, fix errors, retry commit
If pre-push fails: Run `just lint`, `just test-lib`, fix issues, retry push
Format errors: Run `just fmt` to auto-fix, then re-commit
bypass_prevention: FORBIDDEN: `git commit --no-verify` (will be rejected by CI if code is broken)
FORBIDDEN: `git push --no-verify` (CI quality gates are authoritative)
Enforcement: `.git/hooks/pre-commit` and `.git/hooks/pre-push` are symlinked to scripts/hooks/
Rationale: Commits to main bypass the local hook will still fail CI checks, so bypassing is pointless

automation_dimensions.title: CI/CD Gates (GitHub Actions Quality-Gate Pipeline)
automation_dimensions.what_it_is: Automated GitHub Actions workflows triggered on push to main and all PRs. Gates prevent merge until all checks pass.
automation_dimensions.why_it_matters: CI gates are the final authoritative boundary before code reaches main. They run on GitHub infrastructure with exact Rust toolchain (stable + beta), catch platform-specific issues, detect flaky tests, and enforce code organization rules.
trigger_points: On push to main branch (immediate execution)
On pull request open, synchronize, and ready_for_review (blocks merge)
Concurrency: Cancels older runs on the same branch
what_it_validates: File Organization Check (GATE 0):
- No .rs files in root directory
- No .ttl / .rdf files in root (must be in .specify/)
- No .tmpl files in root (must be in crates/*/src/)
- No temporary files (.tmp, .bak, etc.)
Comprehensive Tests (GATE 1):
- Runs on both stable and beta Rust toolchains (future-proofing)
- `cargo test --workspace --tests` (integration tests)
- `cargo test --lib` (unit tests)
- `cargo test --doc` (doctest examples)
Quality Gates / Poka-Yoke (GATE 2):
- Panic Point Detection: Scans for .expect() / .unwrap() in production code
* ALLOWED: .expect() / .unwrap() with `// SAFE:` comment
* FORBIDDEN: Uncommented .expect(), .unwrap(), panic!() in src/
- Code Organization: No files in root, proper crate layout
- All gates required to pass for merge
recovery: If CI fails: Read the GitHub Actions run log (click the red X on the commit)
Panic points detected: Either add `// SAFE: reason` comment or remove the call
File organization error: Move file to correct subdirectory and re-commit
Flaky test: Investigate test logic, use real collaborators (not mocks), rerun
Re-trigger: Push a new commit to the branch (CI runs automatically)
bypass_prevention: FORBIDDEN: Merge PR without CI status check passing (GitHub enforces this)
FORBIDDEN: Push directly to main (GitHub branch protection requires CI + code review)
Enforcement: GitHub branch protection rules (seanchatmangpt/ggen settings)
Requirement: All workflows must report success before 'Merge' button is enabled

automation_dimensions.title: Just Recipes (Cargo Make Task Coordination)
automation_dimensions.what_it_is: Single entry point for all development tasks. 'just' is the command runner; Cargo.toml members are the 15 workspace crates.
automation_dimensions.why_it_matters: Just ensures consistent execution across all contributors. One recipe runs the right tool with the right timeout, avoiding skipped steps and incomplete workflows. Pre-commit, CI, and agent automation all delegate to just recipes.
trigger_points: Manual: `just <task>` from any directory in the repository
Git hooks: Pre-commit.sh and pre-push.sh call `just check`, `just lint`, `just test-lib`
CI workflows: GitHub Actions call `just check`, `just test`, `just lint`
Agent automation: Agents call `just sync`, `just test-unit`, `just pre-commit`
what_it_validates: Compilation & Build:
- `just check`: 60s timeout, `cargo check --workspace` (VITAL for pre-commit)
- `just build`: Debug build of all crates
- `just build-release`: Release binary for ggen-cli
Code Quality:
- `just fmt-check`: 2m check-only (used in pre-commit)
- `just fmt`: Auto-format all code
- `just lint`: 90s Clippy with -D warnings on all targets (VITAL for pre-push)
Testing:
- `just test`: Full suite with 30s hot cache, 120s escalation on first run
- `just test-lib`: Unit tests only <30s (VITAL for pre-push)
- `just test-doc`: Doctest examples <60s or 180s escalation
- `just test-marketplace`: Pack layer validation tests
- `just test-mutation`: Mutation score ≥60% (CI gate)
Pre-Flight & Gates:
- `just timeout-check`: Verifies 'timeout' command exists (required for timed recipes)
- `just pre-commit`: Full pre-commit gate (fmt-check → check → lint → test-lib)
- `just slo-check`: Performance SLOs validation (<15s first build, <2s incremental)
Pipeline:
- `just sync`: Full μ₁-μ₅ pipeline with audit (writes artifacts, receipts)
- `just sync-dry`: Preview sync without writing
recovery: Recipe timeout: First run of large projects may exceed timeout; just escalates automatically
Compilation error: Fix the source, rerun `just check`
Lint failure: Run `just fmt` first, then rerun `just lint`
Test failure: Run `just test-unit` (fast), identify issue, fix, rerun
Pre-commit gate failed: Run each gate manually to isolate the issue
bypass_prevention: FORBIDDEN: Direct `cargo check` instead of `just check` (bypasses timeout, may hang)
FORBIDDEN: Direct `cargo test` instead of `just test` (loses escalation logic, timeout)
FORBIDDEN: Running just recipes in wrong order (must follow dependency DAG)
Enforcement: `.claude/rules/_core/absolute.md` Rule 4: 'ALWAYS just <task> — NEVER direct cargo'
Rationale: Just recipes encode timeouts, escalation logic, and dependency ordering

automation_dimensions.title: MCP Servers (ggen-lsp-mcp & ggen-a2a-mcp Availability & Health)
automation_dimensions.what_it_is: Two MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that expose ggen-lsp repair routes and A2A agent coordination as tools. Servers run over stdio and must be healthy and responsive.
automation_dimensions.why_it_matters: MCP servers are how agents execute repairs, check diagnostics, and coordinate multi-agent workflows. If a server is unavailable or stuck, agent automation cannot repair GGEN-* diagnostic failures, blocking ggen sync.
trigger_points: ggen-lsp-mcp: Started by Claude Code when editing ggen.toml or .ttl files (tool invocations trigger repairs)
ggen-a2a-mcp: Started by agent orchestrators when multi-agent workflows need coordination
Health monitor: Background loop in ggen-a2a-mcp that pings agent endpoints every 5 seconds
what_it_validates: ggen-lsp-mcp Server:
- Availability: Can start and listen on stdio without panicking
- Tools: Exposes 4 repair routes as MCP tools (source_law_repair, security_repair, proof_topology_repair, etc.)
- Diagnostics: Consumers can call `ggen check` to get GGEN-* codes as errors
- Repairs: Each tool accepts a `RoutePlan` and returns a `RouteResult` with the repaired content
ggen-a2a-mcp Server:
- Availability: Can start and listen on stdio
- Agent Registry: Can register agents with endpoint URL and health config
- Health Monitoring: Background task pings each agent every 5 seconds
* Updates HealthStatus::Healthy, ::Degraded, ::Unreachable
- Consecutive Failure Tracking: Counts 3+ consecutive failures as :Unreachable
- Timeout: Health check requests have 5s timeout
recovery: If ggen-lsp-mcp is stuck: Kill the process, restart Claude Code
If health check is failing: Check agent endpoint URL is reachable (curl -I <url>)
If MCP server won't start: Check for tracing_subscriber initialization conflicts, logs for panic details
If routes not available: Ensure ggen-lsp crate compiled successfully (`just check`)
bypass_prevention: FORBIDDEN: Running ggen sync while GGEN-* diagnostics are active (LSP server must fix them first)
FORBIDDEN: Ignoring health monitor alerts (agents marked :Unreachable must be restarted)
Enforcement: ggen-cli sync command checks for active GGEN-* diagnostics before pipeline execution
Rationale: MCP tools are the only way to repair SPARQL/Tera/manifest mismatches; they must be available

automation_dimensions.title: Andon Signals (Stop-the-Line Compiler Errors, Test Failures, Clippy Warnings)
automation_dimensions.what_it_is: Signal detection protocol that halts all work when critical failures appear. Signals are compiler errors (error[E...]), test failures (FAILED), warnings, and Clippy diagnostics.
automation_dimensions.why_it_matters: Andon signals indicate the system is in an invalid state. Continuing work while signals are active results in cascading failures, lost context, and wasted effort. The protocol enforces immediate fix-and-retry.
trigger_points: Compiler error: `cargo check` or `cargo build` returns non-zero exit code
Test failure: `cargo test` reports FAILED count > 0
Clippy warning: `cargo clippy` emits `warning:` (not just `error[...]`)
LSP diagnostics: ggen-lsp server reports GGEN-* codes with ERROR severity
what_it_validates: CRITICAL Level Signals:
- error[E...]: Compiler errors (MUST fix immediately, nothing else exists)
- test ... FAILED: Test failures (MUST read failure, identify root cause, fix)
HIGH Level Signals:
- warning: Any warning in produced code (unacceptable before release)
- Clippy errors: Caught by `just lint` (real bugs, must fix)
CLEAR Level:
- All gates pass: `just timeout-check && just check && just test && just lint && just slo-check`
- Only then: Proceed to next task
recovery: Step 1: Read the signal (compiler error, test failure line number, Clippy rule)
Step 2: Identify root cause (not the symptom, the actual cause)
Step 3: Fix the cause (modify source code)
Step 4: Re-run the check (recompile, retest)
Step 5: Repeat until all signals clear
Tools for systematic fixing:
- TodoWrite: Create 10+ todos covering every signal
- For each todo: Read error → Fix → Verify → Update todo
- Re-run validation gates until all green
bypass_prevention: FORBIDDEN: `#[allow(...)]` to silence warnings (masks the problem)
FORBIDDEN: `#[ignore]` on failing tests (breaks the test contract)
FORBIDDEN: `|| true` to mask command failures (failure becomes invisible)
FORBIDDEN: `unwrap()` to bypass error handling (panics in production)
FORBIDDEN: `todo!()` in committed code (incomplete implementation)
Enforcement: Clippy lints, pre-push hook, CI checks all reject these patterns
Rationale: Suppressing signals doesn't fix problems; it just makes them invisible and worse

automation_dimensions.title: LSP Diagnostics (GGEN-* Codes Emitted by ggen-lsp, Block Sync)
automation_dimensions.what_it_is: Language server diagnostic codes (GGEN-TPL-001, GGEN-OUT-001, GGEN-RULE-001, GGEN-YIELD-001, etc.) that indicate law-surface violations in ggen.toml, SPARQL queries, and Tera templates.
automation_dimensions.why_it_matters: GGEN-* codes are the interface between the ontology and the execution engine. An ERROR-severity diagnostic means the sync pipeline will fail; blocking sync prevents broken artifacts from being written.
trigger_points: File save: User edits ggen.toml, *.sparql, *.tera files
LSP analysis: ggen-lsp analyzer runs against the file
On-demand: `ggen check --with-routes` headless check in CI
what_it_validates: GGEN-TPL-001 (Unbound Projection):
- Severity: ERROR
- Trigger: Tera template references {{ var }} that SPARQL SELECT does not produce
- Detector: analyzers::detect_tpl_001 over ggen.toml + SPARQL
- Repair: source_law_repair route (fix SELECT or Tera)
- Example: {{ row.foo }} with SELECT ?bar, ?baz (foo is unbound)
GGEN-OUT-001 (Unbound Output Path):
- Severity: ERROR
- Trigger: output_file pattern uses {{ var }} that SPARQL does not provide
- Detector: analyzers::detect_out_001 over ggen.toml + SPARQL
- Example: output_file = 'gen/{{ namespace }}/code.rs' but SELECT ?name, ?version
GGEN-RULE-001 (Unbound Rule File):
- Severity: ERROR
- Trigger: {file = ...} references missing file on disk
- Detector: analyzers::detect_rule_001 over ProjectIndex
- Example: {file = 'queries/missing.sparql'} but file does not exist
GGEN-YIELD-001 (Output Path Escape / Layer Violation):
- Severity: ERROR
- Trigger: output_file pattern escapes project root (e.g., ../../../etc/passwd)
- Detector: analyzers::detect_yield_001 checks for path traversal
- Repair: security_repair route (constrain output_file to safe directory)
GGEN-HARNESS-001 (Harness Mismatch):
- Severity: RELEASE_BLOCKING
- Trigger: Cargo.toml [[test]] path does not match proof file on disk
- Detector: analyze_harness_001 over harness_index::HarnessIndex
- Repair: proof_topology_repair route
E0011 / E0013 / E0015 (SPARQL-level diagnostics):
- E0011: CONSTRUCT lacks ORDER BY (WARNING, STRICT_MODE: ERROR)
- E0013: SELECT lacks ORDER BY (WARNING, STRICT_MODE: ERROR)
- E0015: Identity CONSTRUCT (no-op mapping, ADVISORY)
All Species Metadata:
- code: Stable diagnostic code (e.g. 'GGEN-TPL-001')
- severity_policy: 'error', 'warning', 'release_blocking', 'advisory'
- surfaces: Which law surfaces are involved (ggen.toml, SPARQL, Tera, OS, Cargo.toml)
- route: Repair route slug (source_law_repair, security_repair, etc.)
- detector_active: Boolean (true = detector emits this diagnostic; false = metadata only)
recovery: If GGEN-* diagnostic appears:
1. Read the diagnostic code (GGEN-TPL-001, GGEN-OUT-001, etc.)
2. Open ggen-lsp repair route (CodeAction in editor or `ggen check --with-routes`)
3. Accept the repair (editor applies the fix)
4. Verify the diagnostic clears
5. Save the file and re-run ggen sync
If repair route is not available:
1. Check detector_active == true in diagnostic_species.rs
2. Manually fix the issue following the diagnostic guidance
3. Re-save the file
bypass_prevention: FORBIDDEN: Running `ggen sync` with active GGEN-* ERROR diagnostics
Enforcement: ggen-cli sync command checks for active diagnostics before starting pipeline
Exit code: sync returns 1 if diagnostics block execution
Rationale: GGEN-* errors indicate invariant violations; sync would fail anyway, so the check is fail-fast

automation_dimensions.title: Sync Validation Gates (Lockfile, Digests, Profiles Before Artifact Emission)
automation_dimensions.what_it_is: Validation checkpoints in ggen sync that must pass before any artifact is written: lockfile presence, digest verification, profile enforcement.
automation_dimensions.why_it_matters: These gates prevent the pipeline from producing artifacts under invalid conditions (missing packs, profile violation, unverified digests). Without them, sync would silently write broken artifacts.
trigger_points: User command: `ggen sync` is invoked
Pre-pipeline: Before μ₁ (load) stage executes
what_it_validates: Gate A: Lockfile Presence & Reading
- Check: `.ggen/packs.lock` exists and is parseable JSON
- Failure mode: Return Err(MissingLockfile) instead of continuing
- Error code: exit 1
- Why: Lockfile defines the pack set for this sync; missing = undefined behavior
Gate B: Digest Verification
- Check: Each pack's digest in lockfile matches pack TOML on disk
- Failure mode: Return Err(DigestMismatch { pack_id, expected, actual })
- Error code: exit 1
- Why: Digest mismatch means pack was modified after install; cannot trust content
- Invariant: lockfile.digests must be non-empty SHA-256
Gate C: Profile Enforcement (Strict Mode)
- Check: ProfileEnforcer evaluates lockfile.profile against runtime constraints
- Failure mode: If violation, emit DiagnosticReceipt and return Err(ProfileViolation)
- Error code: exit 1 (no artifact emission)
- Why: Profile defines trust tier and runtime requirements; violation = cannot emit
- Invariant: Artifact must not be written if profile constraint is violated
Gate D: GGEN-* Diagnostics
- Check: ggen check runs against ggen.toml, queries, templates
- Failure mode: If any ERROR diagnostic, return Err(DiagnosticsBlocking) and halt
- Error code: exit 1
- Why: ERROR diagnostics indicate law-surface violations; sync would fail anyway
recovery: Lockfile missing: Run `ggen packs add <pack_id>` to install packs (creates lockfile)
Digest mismatch: Reinstall the pack or restore the pack TOML to its installed state
Profile violation: Update pack trust tier or relax profile constraints in ggen.toml
GGEN-* blocking: Use ggen-lsp repair route to fix the diagnostic, then retry sync
bypass_prevention: FORBIDDEN: Skipping digest verification with a flag (no --skip-digests option exists)
FORBIDDEN: Emitting artifacts when profile constraint is violated (ProfileEnforcer enforces)
FORBIDDEN: Continuing sync when GGEN-* ERROR diagnostics are active (sync checks before pipeline)
Enforcement: All gates are hard-coded, no environment variables to skip them
Rationale: Skipping these gates leads to decorative completion (sync reports success but artifact is invalid)

automation_dimensions.title: Receipt Signing (Cryptographic Ed25519 Signatures on All Artifacts)
automation_dimensions.what_it_is: Every ggen sync run produces a signed cryptographic receipt (.ggen/receipts/latest.json) that binds input hashes (ontology, packs, templates) to output hashes (generated files).
automation_dimensions.why_it_matters: Receipts are the proof object that connects source-of-truth to generated artifacts. A receipt with a valid Ed25519 signature proves that: (1) this exact input was processed, (2) this exact output was generated, (3) the process was authorized.
trigger_points: After μ₅ (emit): When all artifacts have been written to disk
Automatic: Every sync run generates a new receipt
what_it_validates: Receipt Structure (JSON):
'operation_id': '<UUID v4, non-zero>',
'timestamp': '<RFC-3339>',
'input_hashes': {
'<pack_id@version>': '<SHA-256 hex>',
'ontology': '<SHA-256 hex>',
'ggen_toml': '<SHA-256 hex>'
'output_hashes': {
'path/to/artifact.rs': '<SHA-256 hex>',
'path/to/other.rs': '<SHA-256 hex>'
'signature': '<base64 Ed25519 signature (non-empty!)>'
Invariants:
- operation_id: Must be a real Uuid::new_v4(), never hardcoded or zero
- timestamp: Must be current RFC-3339, not stale
- input_hashes: Must include every pack consumed at its locked version
- output_hashes: Must include every file written during emit stage
- signature: Must be non-empty base64, signed with private key in .ggen/keys/signing.key
Verification:
- Command: `ggen receipt verify <path/to/receipt.json> --public-key .ggen/keys/public.pem`
- Success: is_valid: true iff signature is valid
- Failure: is_valid: false if signature is empty, malformed, or does not match body
recovery: Empty signature field: Fix the receipt-generation code to call sign() method
Invalid signature: Regenerate the receipt by running sync again
Missing receipt: Check .ggen/receipts/ directory and run sync to create one
Receipt tampering: If receipt body and signature don't match, verify operation_id and input_hashes
bypass_prevention: FORBIDDEN: Emitting artifacts without a signed receipt (μ₅ always generates receipt)
FORBIDDEN: Hardcoding operation_id or timestamp in receipt (must be real values)
FORBIDDEN: Skipping signature generation with env var or flag (always sign)
Enforcement: sync.rs enforces that no artifact is emitted until receipt is signed
Invariant: Contract drift detection — `.ggen/receipts/latest.json` must have non-empty signature
Test: crates/ggen-config/tests/ includes sabotage tests for empty signature field

automation_dimensions.title: Determinism Checks (Build Reproducibility & Hash Verification)
automation_dimensions.what_it_is: Validation that ggen is deterministic: running sync twice with the same inputs produces bit-identical outputs and identical receipt hashes.
automation_dimensions.why_it_matters: Non-determinism indicates hidden state, timing-dependent behavior, or uncontrolled randomness. Determinism is required for reproducible builds, audit trails, and receipt verification.
trigger_points: Continuous: Every sync run must be deterministic
On-demand: `just slo-check` includes determinism validation
what_it_validates: Hash Reproducibility:
- Input setup: Fixed ontology, fixed packs, fixed ggen.toml
- Run 1: Execute ggen sync → receipt_1.json (with input_hashes, output_hashes)
- Run 2: Execute ggen sync again → receipt_2.json
- Check: receipt_1.input_hashes == receipt_2.input_hashes
- Check: receipt_1.output_hashes == receipt_2.output_hashes
- Check: All generated files are byte-identical
Sources of Non-Determinism to Prevent:
- HashMap iteration order (use BTreeMap or explicit sort)
- UUID generation in output (use deterministic IDs)
- Timestamps in generated code (should be derived from receipt, not current time)
- Random numbers without seed (use RNG_SEED env var in tests)
- File modification times (archive paths, use consistent mtime)
Performance SLOs (part of slo-check):
- First build: ≤15 seconds (including dependency fetch)
- Incremental build: ≤2 seconds (cold cache, no changes)
- Determinism validation: Must run without timeout escalation
recovery: Non-deterministic output: Identify which file differs between runs (diff receipt hashes)
Random behavior: Search for Uuid::new_v4() or rand:: calls outside tests
HashMap ordering: Replace HashMap with BTreeMap, add explicit sort()
Timing-dependent: Remove DateTime::now() from generated artifacts
File order: Sort file listing before iteration
bypass_prevention: FORBIDDEN: Using HashMap for iteration in μ₃-μ₅ stages (use BTreeMap)
FORBIDDEN: Calling Uuid::new_v4() in generated artifact content (breaks determinism)
FORBIDDEN: Using current time in generated code (should be in receipt only)
Enforcement: Code review checks for non-deterministic patterns
Test: `tests/determinism_test.rs` verifies two runs produce identical artifacts

automation_dimensions.title: Auto-Merge Gates (Conditions Enabling Auto-Merge to Main)
automation_dimensions.what_it_is: Conditions that must be satisfied before GitHub auto-merge is enabled for a PR. All gates are enforced by branch protection rules on main.
automation_dimensions.why_it_matters: Auto-merge gates ensure that only code passing all quality checks reaches main. Manual merge could bypass critical gates (test failures, linting, security audit).
trigger_points: PR creation: When a PR is opened or updated
CI runs: All checks must report success
Manual approval: Code review must approve (optional, based on settings)
what_it_validates: Required Status Checks (must ALL pass before merge):
1. File Organization Check — passes
2. Comprehensive Tests (stable) — passes
3. Comprehensive Tests (beta) — passes (future-proofing)
4. Quality Gates / Poka-Yoke — passes (panic detection, code organization)
Branch Protection Rules (seanchatmangpt/ggen GitHub settings):
- Required status checks: All above must be green
- Require branches to be up to date: PR must be rebased on main before merge
- Require code reviews: Minimum 1 approval required (can be set to 2 for stricter gates)
- Dismiss stale PR reviews: Old approvals are invalidated if PR is updated
- Allow auto-merge: Can be enabled per-PR by PR author, but only if all checks pass
No Direct Push to Main:
- Enforcement: GitHub rule prevents direct push to main (must use PR)
- Exception: github-actions bot can push (for semantic versioning, releases)
Merge Strategy:
- Default: Squash and merge (one commit per PR, cleaner history)
- Reason: Preserves PR title as commit message, all changes atomic
recovery: If CI fails and PR cannot merge:
1. Read the GitHub Actions run log
2. Fix the issue locally (compile error, test failure, etc.)
3. Push a new commit (CI runs automatically)
4. Once CI passes, merge is allowed
If PR is behind main:
1. GitHub will show 'Update branch' button
2. Click it to rebase PR onto latest main
3. CI re-runs on rebased PR
4. Merge allowed once CI passes
bypass_prevention: FORBIDDEN: Direct push to main (branch protection prevents it)
FORBIDDEN: Merge PR if any required check is failing (GitHub enforces)
FORBIDDEN: Force-push to main to bypass checks (GitHub hook prevents it)
Enforcement: GitHub repository settings (seanchatmangpt/ggen Branch protection rules)
Rationale: All gates must pass; there is no 'quick merge' option that bypasses quality checks

cross_dimensional_dependencies.description: How the 10 dimensions work together to enforce complete automation
flow: Pre-commit hook runs: just check, just fmt-check (Dimension 3)
If fails: Stop, fix, retry commit (Dimension 5 Andon protocol)
Pre-push hook runs: just check, just lint, just test-lib (Dimension 3)
If fails: Stop, fix, retry push (Dimension 5 Andon protocol)
If any CI check fails: PR is blocked (cannot merge)
- Repeat until CI passes (Dimension 5 Andon protocol)
All CI checks pass: Merge button enabled (Dimension 10 gates satisfied)
Code review approval (if required by branch protection)
Merge to main (auto-merge enabled or manual click)
- All tests must pass on main after merge
- If main build broken: Immediate rollback or hotfix (Andon signal: STOP)

pre_commit.purpose: Catch compilation errors before creating commit

pre_push.purpose: Catch linting, tests, and compilation before sharing code

gates: File organization check (no root files, proper crate layout)

ci_tests.purpose: Verify all tests pass on CI infrastructure

ci_quality.purpose: Prevent dangerous patterns from reaching main

gates: PR must be up to date with main
branch_protection.purpose: Prevent merge if any gate is failing

scenario_1.name: Compilation error in pre-commit
scenario_1.signal: error[E0425]: cannot find value `foo` in this scope

response.step1: Pre-commit hook exits 1, commit is rejected
response.step2: Developer reads error message (file:line where undefined)
response.step3: Fix the code (define `foo` or use correct identifier)

response.step1: Pre-push hook exits 1, push is rejected
response.step3: Reads test failure output (assertion that failed)
response.step4: Identifies root cause (logic bug, missing setup, etc.)

response.step1: CI check reports warning, merge is blocked
response.step3: Fixes warning (remove variable or use it)

scenario_4.name: GGEN-TPL-001 diagnostic blocks sync
scenario_4.signal: GGEN-TPL-001: Template uses {{ var }} that SELECT does not produce

response.step2: Developer opens ggen-lsp repair route (CodeAction in editor)
response.step3: Accepts the repair (adds missing SELECT column or removes {{ var }})
response.step4: Reruns ggen sync (diagnostic no longer blocks)

scenario_5.name: Non-deterministic output detected
scenario_5.signal: Receipt hash differs on second sync run

response.step2: Diff receipts to find which file differs
response.step3: Search for random sources (HashMap, Uuid::new_v4(), DateTime::now())
response.step4: Replace non-deterministic code with deterministic version
response.step5: Verify second sync produces identical artifacts

what_prevents_bypass: Git hooks are locally enforced (can be skipped with --no-verify, but CI gates catch broken code)
CI gates are enforced by GitHub branch protection (cannot merge if checks fail)
Just recipes encode timeouts and escalation logic (cannot be bypassed, running cargo directly loses safety)
MCP servers are required for LSP repair routes (agents cannot fix GGEN-* without them)
Andon signals are enforced by pre-commit/pre-push hooks and CI (must fix, cannot suppress)
Sync gates are hard-coded (no environment variables to skip lockfile, digest, or profile checks)
Receipt signatures are Ed25519 (cannot forge without private key)
Branch protection rules are GitHub-enforced (cannot push directly to main)
what_makes_faking_impossible: Tests must use real collaborators (Chicago TDD only, no mocks, no test doubles)
Receipts must have non-empty signature (codebase assertion checks this)
Artifacts must be written to disk (observable state, verifiable with `stat`)
Digest verification happens before sync (pack TOML hash must match lockfile)
GGEN-* diagnostics are checked before pipeline (cannot emit artifacts with active errors)
Determinism checks are automated (two runs must produce identical outputs)
Andon signals are non-suppressible (compiler errors cannot be ignored)

before_every_commit: ✓ Read error output (do not ignore warnings or errors)
before_every_push: ✓ Run `git push` (pre-push hook runs full tier: check + lint + test-lib)
✓ Read hook output (do not ignore any failures)
✓ Fix issues, rerun `just test-lib`, retry push
before_every_merge: ✓ All CI checks pass (file organization, tests, quality gates)
✓ PR is up to date with main
before_every_release: ✓ Main branch CI is green (all checks pass)
✓ GitHub Actions release workflow creates artifacts (sha256 checksums)
✓ Update Homebrew formula (manual step for now)

revision_history: 2026-06-14 v1.0: Initial Definition of Done (10 dimensions, 26.5.28)
notes.next_review: 2026-07-14 (assess completeness of MCP health monitoring, auto-merge coverage)
